text: Artificial intelligence (AI) has been developed and deployed for years but has made a sudden arrival in the limelight. Most of the recent advancements were introduced by Google, Microsoft and Meta and so on. Open AI, recently, shocked the world with an AI application named “ChatGPT” which is a type of Large Language Model (LLM) based on the Transformer architecture. It has been trained on a massive dataset of human language and it learns to generate human-sounding responses. A ChatGPT application uses deep learning algorithms to generate natural and human-sounding language that is proportionate to an input or text. The model has a large number of parameters, it supports contextual and open-domain generation, therefore, it can be used in numerous applications such as chatbots and customer support systems, language translation services and so on. The ChatGPT application can have a dramatic impact on several sectors of the society and everyday life. However, its application also raises several ethical, social and even employment concerns that have to be addressed in order to use ChatGPT to its maximum potential. The paper gives a first overview of ChatGPT by describing in detail its architecture and its training. Moreover, it also outlines the positive and negative impacts of ChatGPT on society. It also gives recommendations on how to incorporate technology, policy, education and ethics to ensure that ChatGPT has the least amount of negative side effects. This paper is an initial investigation into ChatGPT which aims to deepen the understanding of the ChatGPT application and its predicted social impact.
